display.purpose: Headlines, empty state titles, section dividers.

body.purpose: Primary UI text, chat messages, descriptions. 15px for mobile/desktop parity.

label.purpose: Buttons, labels, metadata, timestamps. Slightly tracked out for clarity.

mono.purpose: Code blocks, terminal output, file paths, technical data. 13px with 1.6 line-height.

shadows.name: ambient-modal
shadows.purpose: Modal dialogs, settings panels when opened.

shadows.name: focus-glow
shadows.purpose: Focus-visible states on inputs and interactive elements.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.purpose: Mobile breakpoint - bottom sheet nav, stacked layout.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Tablet breakpoint - sidebar collapses to narrow.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Desktop breakpoint - full sidebar, dual panel.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary actions - send message, save settings, confirm dialogs.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #7AA2F7; color: #0F111A; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 6px; border: none; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #7DCFFF; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7AA2F7; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary actions - cancel, close, less prominent choices.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #C0CAF5; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #131629; color: #C0CAF5; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7AA2F7; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tool Card
components.description: Collapsible tool activity group in chat trail.
components.html:
⚡Bash Command▼
ls -la
components.css: .ds-card-tool { background: #131629; border: 1px solid #31344a; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; margin: 4px 0; } .ds-tool-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 6px 10px; background: #131629; border-bottom: 1px solid #31344a; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-tool-header:hover { background: #171b30; } .ds-tool-title { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #C0CAF5; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tool-icon { font-size: 12px; } .ds-tool-expand { font-size: 10px; color: #6a739d; margin-left: auto; } .ds-tool-body { padding: 10px 12px; } .ds-tool-output { background: #171b30; border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px 10px; margin: 0; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.6; color: #C0CAF5; overflow-x: auto; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Search fields in file tree, command palette, settings filter.
components.css: .ds-input-search { background: #131629; color: #C0CAF5; padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid #31344a; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', Consolas, monospace; font-size: 12px; width: 100%; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-input-search::placeholder { color: #45496F; font-style: italic; } .ds-input-search:focus { outline: none; border-color: #7AA2F7; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(122,162,247,0.15); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Navigation items in sidebar - sessions, settings, file tree toggle.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 5px 10px; background: transparent; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', Consolas, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #6a739d; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #131629; color: #C0CAF5; } .ds-nav-active { background: #131629; color: #C0CAF5; } .ds-nav-icon { font-size: 14px; } .ds-nav-label { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Chat Message Label
components.description: Bold label for chat messages - 'You', 'AI', timestamps.
components.css: .ds-chat-label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-chat-author { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #C0CAF5; } .ds-chat-time { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', Consolas, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #45496F; }

components.name: Code Block
components.description: Markdown-rendered code blocks with syntax highlighting.
components.css: .ds-code-block { background: #131629; border: 1px solid #292C43; border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 12px; margin: 8px 0; overflow-x: auto; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', Consolas, monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.6; color: #C0CAF5; } .ds-code-block .token-keyword { color: #7AA2F7; } .ds-code-block .token-function { color: #7DCFFF; } .ds-code-block .token-string { color: #9ECE6A; } .ds-code-block .token-number { color: #E0AF68; } .ds-code-block .token-comment { color: #45496F; font-style: italic; }

narrative.overview:
A spunky terminal-inspired chat interface that feels alive. OpenKot is where playful experimentation meets agentic AI — the terminal isn't just a panel, it's a personality. Micro-interactions, smooth transitions, and tactile feedback make every tap feel deliberate. The design rejects the sterile 'AI wrapper' aesthetic in favor of something that feels hand-crafted, inviting, and unpretentious.

This system explicitly rejects generic AI wrapper tropes: no glassmorphism, no gradient text, no hero metrics with big numbers. It also avoids corporate SaaS darkness (navy blue, cards everywhere) and hacker/cyberpunk neon-on-black overload. Instead, it draws from cool tech dark themes (Tokyo Night, Nord) — blues and slate tones that feel like a focused night-coding session.
keyCharacteristics: Chat-first, not tool-first — conversation is the hero
Multi-device parity — desktop and mobile get equal design attention
Tactile and confident — clear surfaces, visible borders, distinct interactive states
Spunky personality — micro-interactions and delight in small details
Cool tech dark — blues and slate, never navy corporate or neon hacker

rules.name: The Chat-First Rule
rules.body: The accent color (Electric Blue) is used on ≤15% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point — when it appears, it means something. Overusing it makes the interface feel like a generic AI wrapper.

rules.name: The No-Gradient Rule
rules.body: No gradient text, no gradient backgrounds, no gradient borders. Solid colors only. If depth is needed, use tonal layering (bg-2, bg-3) or shadows.

rules.name: The Mono-For-Data Rule
rules.body: Any technical content (code, paths, terminal, diffs) uses IBM Plex Mono. Any conversational content uses IBM Plex Sans. No exceptions — mixing sans into code or mono into chat breaks the personality.

rules.name: The 15px Body Rule
rules.body: Body text is always 15px, never 14px (too small for mobile) or 16px (too large for code-dense panels). This size works across desktop and mobile without scaling.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat until proven otherwise. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, elevation, focus). A card doesn't get a shadow just by existing.

rules.name: The Tonal-Over-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth is conveyed through background tonal steps (bg → bg-2 → bg-3), not shadows. This keeps the cool tech dark palette cohesive and avoids the 'floating cards' SaaS look.

dos: Do use Electric Blue (#7AA2F7) sparingly — it's an accent, not a brand wash.
Do maintain 6px or 8px radius consistently — no mixing 4px and 12px on the same surface.
Do use IBM Plex Mono for all code, paths, terminal output, and technical data.
Do ensure touch targets are minimum 44px on mobile — buttons, nav items, chips.
Do use tonal layering (bg → bg-2 → bg-3) to show depth, not shadows.
Do keep chat messages flat and left-aligned — no speech bubbles, no avatar circles.
Do make the chat the hero — tools, editors, and terminals support the conversation, never overshadow it.
donts: Don't use gradient text, gradient backgrounds, or gradient borders. Solid colors only.
Don't use glassmorphism, blur effects, or semi-transparent surfaces. This isn't a generic AI wrapper.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left greater than 1px) as colored accents. Use background tints or nothing.
Don't create identical card grids (same-sized cards with icon + heading + text, repeated). Vary sizes or use lists.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number, small label, gradient accent). Chat is the hero, not metrics.
Don't use em dashes (—) in any UI copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't wrap everything in cards. Most things don't need one — use bg-2 or bg-3 directly.
Don't use modal as first thought. Try inline expansion, progressive disclosure, or bottom sheet (mobile) first.
Don't animate CSS layout properties (width, height, margin, padding). Use transforms or opacity only.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Ease out with exponential curves (ease-out-quart/quint/expo) for state transitions.
